task_completion.description: Vital Recorder application is open and active
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Vital Recorder application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The specified case file is loaded into the application
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Vital Recorder interface shows a loaded case, with 'case_0018.vital' visible in the title bar, recent files, or data loading area.

task_completion.description: The application timeline is navigated to the 30-minute mark
task_completion.visual_evidence: The time indicator, playback cursor, or timeline in Vital Recorder explicitly displays '00:30:00' or shows the cursor positioned at the 30-minute mark.

task_completion.description: At least two physiological tracks are displayed
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main waveform display area of Vital Recorder contains at least two distinct physiological data tracks (e.g., labeled ART, ECG, SpO2, etc.) with visible waveforms.

task_completion.description: The screenshot is saved to the specified Desktop path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file icon named 'case_snapshot.png' is visible on the Desktop, or a file explorer window shows this file successfully created in 'C:\Users\Docker\Desktop'.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the Vital Recorder GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent actively using the Vital Recorder application interface (e.g., clicking menus, dragging the timeline) rather than just typing commands in a terminal.

integrity.description: The output file is a genuine image, not a renamed text file
integrity.visual_evidence: The created file 'case_snapshot.png' is treated as an image file by the OS (e.g., shows an image thumbnail or opens in an image viewer).

integrity.description: Agent did not use scripts to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence in the terminal or editor of the agent using scripts (like Python or ImageMagick) to artificially generate or download the screenshot without actually capturing the Vital Recorder interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is 'C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\case_snapshot.png'. The target time in the application must be 00:30:00 (30 minutes into the surgical recording). The screenshot should be a valid image file (typically >50KB, at least 800x400 resolution) showing the Vital Recorder interface with at least two physiological tracks.
